short_description.value: Panopticon is an any-sensor foundation model for Earth Observation, extending DINOv2 with multi-sensor view generation, spectral subsampling, and cross-attention over channels, enabling sensor-agnostic processing of multispectral, hyperspectral, and SAR data.

pretext_training_type.value: Self-supervised learning with multi-view distillation (DINOv2 framework) and spectral progressive pre-training

masking_strategy.value: Spectral subsampling/cropping and spatial cropping; metainfo-aware masking

pretraining.value: Two-stage progressive pre-training: first on fMoW, then on all datasets (fMoW, SatlasPretrain, MMEarth, SpectralEarth), with spectral and spatial augmentations, cross-attention patch embedding, and spectral positional embeddings.

value.value: Cross-attention over channels in patch embedding
Wider embedding dimension in channel attention

dataset.value: fMoW (Functional Map of the World, including fMoW-Sentinel)

sampling.value: Random sampling of snapshots per footprint; random channel selection

dataset.value: All datasets: fMoW, SatlasPretrain, MMEarth, SpectralEarth

sampling.value: Random sampling of snapshots per footprint; random channel selection
